name: Mexican Corn Soup (Courtesy Gourmet Magazine)
recipeIngredient: Salt and freshly ground black pepper
3 1/2 cups chicken stock or canned chicken broth
8 ears corn, shucked, kernels cut off the cobs, and cobs reserved separately
1 red bell pepper, roasted and chopped fine
3 tablespoons minced fresh coriander or parsley leaves, or to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. In a kettle, cook the garlic in oil over moderately low heat, stirring for 1 minute.
2. Add onion and jalapeno peppers; cook until onion is softened (about 3 minutes).
3. Stir in cumin, ground coriander seed, salt, and pepper; cook for 2 minutes.
4. Add carrot and celery; cook over moderate heat, stirring, for 5 minutes.
5. Add stock, corn cobs, and 2 1/2 cups water. Simmer for 15 minutes.
6. Stir in all but 1 cup of reserved corn kernels; simmer until kernels are very tender (12-15 minutes), discard cobs.
7. In a blender, puree batches on high speed for at least 1 minute or until smooth. For an even smoother soup, force through a fine sieve over a bowl.
8. Let the soup cool to room temperature.
9. Boil remaining corn kernels in boiling water for 2-4 minutes; drain and refresh under cold water.
10. Stir into soup with roasted bell pepper, coriander, cayenne, and season with salt and pepper.
11. Serve at room temperature, garnished with fried tortilla strips.
Yield: About 6 cups, serving 6
